panels.title: RPS (Requests Per Second)
panels.description: Request throughput averaged over 5 minutes. Measures the rate of incoming requests per second across all selected models.

panels.title: RPM (Requests Per Minute)
panels.description: Request throughput averaged over 5 minutes, expressed as requests per minute. Useful for comparing against rate limits. Equivalent to RPS × 60.

panels.title: TPS (Tokens Per Second)
panels.description: Output token throughput averaged over 5 minutes. Measures how fast the proxy is generating completion tokens per second across all selected models.

panels.title: TPM (Tokens Per Minute)
panels.description: Output token throughput averaged over 5 minutes, expressed as tokens per minute. Useful for comparing against provider TPM rate limits. Equivalent to TPS × 60.

panels.title: Total Requests
panels.description: Total requests (successful + failed) across all selected models and deployments.

panels.title: Successful Requests
panels.description: Cumulative count of requests that completed successfully with a non-error response from the upstream LLM provider.

panels.title: Failed Requests
panels.description: Cumulative count of requests that failed with an error response. Includes upstream provider errors, timeouts, rate limits, and any other failure conditions.

panels.title: Error Rate
panels.description: Cumulative percentage of requests that failed out of total completed requests (successful + failed) since metrics collection began. Formula: failed / (successful + failed) × 100. Thresholds: green (<1%) → yellow (1–5%) → red (>5%).

panels.title: In-Flight Requests
panels.description: Number of requests currently being processed (started but not yet completed). A consistently high value may indicate the proxy is saturated or upstream responses are slow.

panels.title: Request Queue Time (P95)
panels.description: 95th percentile time requests spend waiting in the proxy's internal queue before being dispatched to the upstream LLM. Rising queue time indicates the proxy is receiving requests faster than it can process them.

panels.title: E2E Request Latency (P50/P95/P99)
panels.description: End-to-end request latency from the moment the proxy receives the request to when the full response is returned. Includes proxy overhead, queue time, and the upstream LLM call. P50 = median, P95 = 95th percentile, P99 = 99th percentile (worst 1% of requests).

panels.title: LLM API Latency (P50/P95/P99)
panels.description: Upstream LLM API call latency only — the time spent waiting for the model provider (Huawei MaaS) to respond. Excludes proxy overhead and queue time. Useful for isolating provider-side slowdowns from proxy-side issues.

panels.title: P95 Latency
panels.description: 95th percentile end-to-end request latency. Thresholds: green (<1s) → yellow (1–5s) → red (>5s). If this gauge enters the red zone, 5% of users are experiencing >5s response times.

panels.title: P99 Latency
panels.description: 99th percentile end-to-end request latency. Thresholds: green (<2s) → yellow (2–10s) → red (>10s). Represents the worst 1% of request latencies.

panels.title: TTFT (Time To First Token)
panels.description: Streaming only. Time from when the API call starts to when the first token of the response is received. This is the 'time to first byte' equivalent for LLMs — critical for perceived responsiveness in streaming chat. Formula: completion_start_time - api_call_start_time.

panels.title: TPOT (Time Per Output Token)
panels.description: Average time spent generating each output token. Applies to both streaming and non-streaming requests. Formula: total_latency / output_tokens. Lower is better — indicates raw generation speed of the model.

panels.title: ITL (Inter-Token Latency)
panels.description: Streaming only. Average time between successive tokens during streaming output. Formula: (end_time - completion_start_time) / (output_tokens - 1). Spikes in ITL indicate provider-side throttling, rate limiting, or network jitter mid-stream.

panels.title: Total Input Tokens
panels.description: Total number of input tokens processed across all selected models.

panels.title: Total Output Tokens
panels.description: Total number of output tokens generated across all selected models.

panels.title: Input Tokens Per Second
panels.description: Rate of input (prompt) tokens being consumed per second, broken down by model. Shows which models receive the most prompt traffic.

panels.title: Output Tokens Per Second
panels.description: Rate of output (completion) tokens being generated per second, broken down by model. Shows which models produce the most output.

panels.title: Input Tokens Per Minute
panels.description: Rate of input (prompt) tokens consumed per minute, broken down by model. Useful for comparing against provider input TPM rate limits and quota tracking.

panels.title: Output Tokens Per Minute
panels.description: Rate of output (completion) tokens generated per minute, broken down by model. Useful for comparing against provider output TPM rate limits and quota tracking.

panels.title: Spend Per Minute
panels.description: USD spent per minute, broken down by model. Shows the actual cost rate at each point in time — useful for spotting cost spikes and tracking spend patterns.

panels.title: Total Spend
panels.description: Cumulative total spend in USD across all selected models since metrics collection began. Use this to track actual cost against budget.

panels.title: Deployment State
panels.description: Deployment state with model_id for unique identification. Shows deployments that have been used (have metrics).

panels.title: Deployments Per Model
panels.description: Number of unique deployments (by model_id) per model. Shows active deployments with metrics.

panels.title: Deployment Cooldown Events
panels.description: Number of deployments currently in cooldown (failed too many times). Should be 0 in a healthy setup.

panels.title: Request Distribution Across Deployments
panels.description: Request distribution across individual deployments (identified by model_id).

panels.title: Per-Deployment Latency (P95)
panels.description: P95 latency per unique deployment (by model_id).
